audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Joshua / Yehoshua Levitical city distribution and rest-summary register, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Levite petition, Moses-command fulfillment, Kohath/Gershon/Merari lots, priestly cities, refuge cities, pasturelands, forty-eight-city total, and fulfillment/rest formula.

verses.restored: Heads of fathers' houses of Levites drew near to Eleazar the priest, Joshua son of Nun, and heads of fathers' houses of tribes of children of Israel.
They spoke to them at Shiloh in land of Canaan, saying, YHWH commanded by hand of Moses to give us cities to dwell in, with their pasturelands for our livestock.
Children of Israel gave to Levites from their inheritance, according to mouth of YHWH, these cities and their pasturelands.
Lot came out for clans of Kohathites; sons of Aaron the priest, from Levites, had by lot thirteen cities from tribe of Judah, tribe of Simeon, and tribe of Benjamin.
Remaining sons of Kohath had by lot ten cities from clans of tribe of Ephraim, tribe of Dan, and half tribe of Manasseh.
Sons of Gershon had by lot thirteen cities from clans of tribe of Issachar, tribe of Asher, tribe of Naphtali, and half tribe of Manasseh in Bashan.
Sons of Merari according to their clans had twelve cities from tribe of Reuben, tribe of Gad, and tribe of Zebulun.
Children of Israel gave these cities and their pasturelands by lot to Levites, as YHWH commanded by hand of Moses.
They gave from tribe of sons of Judah and from tribe of sons of Simeon these cities that are called by name.
They belonged to sons of Aaron, from clans of Kohathites, from sons of Levi, for theirs was first lot.
They gave them Kiriath-arba, father of Anak, that is Hebron, in hill country of Judah, with its pasturelands around it.
But field of city and its villages they gave to Caleb son of Jephunneh as his possession.
To sons of Aaron the priest they gave city of refuge for manslayer, Hebron with its pasturelands, Libnah with its pasturelands,
Jattir with its pasturelands, Eshtemoa with its pasturelands,
Holon with its pasturelands, Debir with its pasturelands,
Ain with its pasturelands, Juttah with its pasturelands, and Beth-shemesh with its pasturelands: nine cities from those two tribes.
From tribe of Benjamin: Gibeon with its pasturelands, Geba with its pasturelands,
Anathoth with its pasturelands, and Almon with its pasturelands: four cities.
All cities of sons of Aaron, the priests, were thirteen cities with their pasturelands.
For clans of sons of Kohath, Levites remaining from sons of Kohath, cities of their lot were from tribe of Ephraim.
They gave them city of refuge for manslayer, Shechem with its pasturelands in hill country of Ephraim, Gezer with its pasturelands,
Kibzaim with its pasturelands, and Beth-horon with its pasturelands: four cities.
From tribe of Dan: Elteke with its pasturelands, Gibbethon with its pasturelands,
Aijalon with its pasturelands, Gath-rimmon with its pasturelands: four cities.
From half tribe of Manasseh: Taanach with its pasturelands and Gath-rimmon with its pasturelands: two cities.
All cities were ten with their pasturelands for clans of remaining sons of Kohath.
To sons of Gershon, from clans of Levites, from half tribe of Manasseh they gave city of refuge for manslayer, Golan in Bashan with its pasturelands, and Beeshterah with its pasturelands: two cities.
From tribe of Issachar: Kishion with its pasturelands, Daberath with its pasturelands,
Jarmuth with its pasturelands, En-gannim with its pasturelands: four cities.
From tribe of Asher: Mishal with its pasturelands, Abdon with its pasturelands,
Helkath with its pasturelands, and Rehob with its pasturelands: four cities.
From tribe of Naphtali: city of refuge for manslayer, Kedesh in Galilee with its pasturelands, Hammoth-dor with its pasturelands, and Kartan with its pasturelands: three cities.
All cities of Gershonites according to their clans were thirteen cities with their pasturelands.
To clans of sons of Merari, remaining Levites, from tribe of Zebulun: Jokneam with its pasturelands, Kartah with its pasturelands,
Dimnah with its pasturelands, Nahalal with its pasturelands: four cities.
From tribe of Reuben: Bezer with its pasturelands, Jahaz with its pasturelands,
Kedemoth with its pasturelands, and Mephaath with its pasturelands: four cities.
From tribe of Gad: city of refuge for manslayer, Ramoth in Gilead with its pasturelands, Mahanaim with its pasturelands,
Heshbon with its pasturelands, Jazer with its pasturelands: all cities four.
All cities for sons of Merari according to their clans, remaining from clans of Levites, were by their lot twelve cities.
All cities of Levites in midst of possession of children of Israel were forty-eight cities with their pasturelands.
These cities were each city with its pasturelands around it; thus it was for all these cities.
YHWH gave to Israel all land he swore to give to their fathers, and they possessed it and dwelt in it.
YHWH gave them rest around, according to all he swore to their fathers; no man from all their enemies stood before them; YHWH gave all their enemies into their hand.
Not one word fell from all good word YHWH spoke to house of Israel; all came.

translation_notes: Levites initiate the petition by invoking Moses' command, showing that landless religious servants must still ask for concrete housing and pasturelands.
The cities are distributed among priestly Aaronites and other Kohathites, Gershonites, and Merarites across multiple tribes, dispersing Levitical presence rather than creating one territorial bloc.
Refuge cities are embedded inside the Levitical network, joining teaching/service with asylum and due process.
Pasturelands matter; nonterritorial inheritance does not mean disembodied spirituality or freedom from material needs.
City lists and totals vary in some textual traditions and require comparison with Chronicles and other witnesses.

The fulfillment formula in verses 43-45 is the book's theological summary and must be read in tension with the same book's repeated admissions of remaining peoples, forced labor, and unconquered land.
'Rest around' describes the narrator's covenant claim from Israel's perspective, not the full lived reality of displaced, subordinated, or surviving communities.
safety_note: Do not use Joshua 21 to deny material support to religious workers, erase refugees by spiritualizing refuge, or claim that fulfillment language cancels the suffering of conquered peoples. Center housing, pasture, distributed service, asylum, and the need to read triumph summaries beside unresolved human realities.
